sections_extracted.extracted_content_summary: Cambridge IGCSE Accounting 0452, Version 1, September 2020, valid for 2023-2025.
Five aims covering knowledge, concepts/principles, numeracy/literacy, accuracy/logic, advanced-study foundation.
Seven sections: fundamentals; sources/recording; verification; procedures; financial statements; analysis/interpretation; principles/policies.
All candidates take Paper 1 (MCQ, 1h15m, 35 marks, 30%) and Paper 2 (structured written, 1h45m, 100 marks, 70%). Grades A*-G.
AO1 Knowledge and understanding (65%), AO2 Analysis (25%), AO3 Evaluation (10%); per-paper weightings listed.

Topics 1.1-7.2 with bullet-point learning outcomes; includes explicit exclusions (e.g., folio columns, three-column running balance, partnership admission/dissolution, cumulative preference shares, incomplete records limited to sole traders).
Formulas for profitability, liquidity, turnover ratios.
Paper 1 and Paper 2 format, duration, marks, AO coverage, calculators allowed.
Terminology mapping current vs previous Cambridge usage.

sections_not_extracted.section: What else you need to know (Before you start / Making entries / After the exam / How students and teachers can use the grades)
Changes to this syllabus for 2023, 2024 and 2025

split_recommendations.assessment_json: Aims, Paper 1/2 structure, AO weightings, command words (p.5-8, p.17-19).
split_recommendations.references: Accounting ratios appendix, terminology table (p.16, p.19-20).

known_risks: Single-channel text extraction; visual review pending.
Official URL and retrieved_at not captured in local archive metadata.
Grade descriptions are noted as 'will be published after first assessment in 2020' and are not present in this PDF.
notes: Extraction completed without copying official PDF text. Page-map mappings use physical PDF page numbers, which align with footer page numbers.
